classifications.reasoning: Confirmed call for Tuesday 2pm. Volunteered a new pain point (no online booking) independently — strong buying signal. 'We lose people all the time' = felt pain acknowledged unprompted.
classifications.suggested_action: Confirm Tuesday 2pm call. Add online booking gap to audit. This is a high-probability conversion — prepare a two-option proposal (SSL+mobile fix vs. full redesign with booking).
classifications.new_pain_point_discovered: No online booking system — self-reported lost customers

classifications.reasoning: Asking whether fixes can be bundled into a redesign — this is a buyer's question, not a fence-sitter's. 'Sort the whole thing in one go' = signalling intent and budget appetite for a larger project.
classifications.suggested_action: Reply today: yes, bundle as one job. Give a redesign + SSL + mobile package price (£500-700 all-in). Mention that bundling is better value and you can move quickly. Confirm his 1-month timeline.
classifications.upsell_note: May convert at full redesign level (~£600) rather than just SSL fix (£150). Do not anchor low — let him confirm the scope.

classifications.reasoning: Acknowledged the pain unprompted ('I've been meaning to sort that for ages'), confirmed the mobile workflow problem (customers told to ring), and asked for pricing — three clear buying signals in one reply.
classifications.suggested_action: Reply with a short pricing note (quote form add-on: £100-150). Offer to show a live example from a similar carpentry business. Ask if Tuesday or Wednesday works for a 15-min call.

classifications.reasoning: Polite but clear rejection — 'happy with how things are', 'not looking to make any changes'. No future-date qualifier. Treat as closed.
classifications.suggested_action: Stop all follow-ups. Mark lead as LOST. No suppression needed (they did not request removal, just declined) — can re-approach in 6 months if circumstances change.

classifications.reasoning: Standard OOO auto-reply. Returns Monday 6 April. No explicit interest or rejection signal.
classifications.suggested_action: Resume follow-up sequence from Tuesday 7 April. David will see the Day 7 email (about Google rankings) when he returns Monday — give him a day before following up.

summary.notes: Strong day — 3 INTERESTED from 5 replies (60% positive rate). Lead 1 (Tom) newly interested after Day 7 follow-up validates the new-angle strategy. Lead 22 (Rob) showing upsell potential to full redesign.
